_schema: Stable meeting-transcript fixtures for Sidebar. Stream `segments` in order (respecting `t`/`ms`) to emulate streaming ASR + live inference. Per segment: t=ms offset from meeting start, speaker, ms=spoken duration, partials?=progressive ASR partials shown before the final, text=final transcript. `expect`=ground-truth agent behaviour for that turn, usable as gold labels when evaluating model output: expect.router matches the router_decision schema; expect.summary matches meeting_summary (the full rolling summary AFTER this turn); expect.prototype.build names the canned prototype the hero agent should produce (intent + uses_screen mirror the router); expect.factcheck is the fact-check verdict. Segments without `expect` are pure chatter the router should skip.

segments.speaker: Maya
segments.text: Alright, Q3 planning. The big problem: we track sprint work in a spreadsheet and nobody actually looks at it.

segments.speaker: Dev
segments.text: Right, it's invisible. Half the team doesn't know what's in flight.

summary.tldr: Team wants a more visible way to track Q3 sprint work than the current spreadsheet.

segments.speaker: Priya
segments.text: We need something visual. A board, columns, cards you can drag.

segments.speaker: Maya
partials: what if we built a kanban board with drag and
what if we built a kanban board with drag-and-drop and a
segments.text: What if we built a kanban board with drag-and-drop and a burndown chart?

prototype.intent: Kanban board with drag-and-drop columns and a sprint burndown chart

summary.tldr: Team is building a visual kanban board with a burndown to replace the sprint spreadsheet.
decisions: Replace the sprint spreadsheet with a kanban board

action_items.task: Prototype a drag-and-drop board with a burndown chart

open_questions: Self-host the board or adopt an existing tool?

prototype.intent: Kanban board with drag-and-drop columns and a sprint burndown chart

segments.speaker: Dev
segments.text: Yes — To Do, In Progress, Done, and the burndown so we can see if we're on track.

segments.speaker: Priya
segments.text: Can you make the cards match the mockup I've got on screen?

prototype.intent: Restyle the board cards to match the on-screen mockup
Restyle the board cards to match the on-screen mockup

segments.speaker: Maya
segments.text: Perfect. Let's make this the Q3 board of record.

summary.tldr: Replacing the Q3 sprint spreadsheet with a visual kanban board + burndown; it becomes the board of record.
decisions: Replace the sprint spreadsheet with a kanban board
Make the new board the Q3 board of record

action_items.task: Apply the on-screen mockup styling to the cards

open_questions: Self-host the board or adopt an existing tool?

segments.speaker: Sam
segments.text: Growth review. Numbers first — where are we on MRR and churn?

segments.speaker: Raj
segments.text: MRR is at 142k, up 9% month over month. Churn ticked up to 4.1%.

summary.tldr: Monthly growth review: MRR $142k (+9% MoM), churn up to 4.1%.
open_questions: Why did churn rise to 4.1% this month?

segments.speaker: Lena
segments.text: Churn's the worry. I want to watch it weekly, not dig through sheets.

segments.speaker: Sam
partials: can we get a dashboard with MRR, churn and
can we get a dashboard — MRR, churn, and a weekly active
segments.text: Can we get a dashboard — MRR, churn, and a weekly active users chart?

prototype.intent: Metrics dashboard showing MRR, churn, and a weekly active users chart

summary.tldr: Building a live metrics dashboard (MRR, churn, WAU) so growth is visible weekly.
decisions: Build a metrics dashboard for MRR, churn, and WAU

action_items.task: Pipe real MRR/churn/WAU data into the dashboard

open_questions: Why did churn rise to 4.1% this month?

prototype.intent: Metrics dashboard showing MRR, churn, and a weekly active users chart

segments.speaker: Raj
segments.text: I'll pipe the real numbers in later. WAU's been climbing, around 12k.

segments.speaker: Lena
segments.text: Our churn is industry-leading though — under 2%, best in the category.

claims: Company churn is under 2% and best in category

factcheck.claim: Our churn is under 2% — best in the category
factcheck.source: internal metrics — Raj reported 4.1% on this call

segments.speaker: Sam
segments.text: Ha — let's keep the dashboard honest, then. Ship it on the team page.

summary.tldr: Shipping an honest metrics dashboard (MRR, churn, WAU) to the team page.
decisions: Build a metrics dashboard for MRR, churn, and WAU
Put it on the team page

action_items.task: Pipe real MRR/churn/WAU data into the dashboard

open_questions: Why did churn rise to 4.1% this month?
